articles.title: EVO Entertainment Breaks Ground on $50M Clearwater Complex
articles.summary: A downtown Clearwater site has been cleared for a $50 million EVO Entertainment complex, signaling continued large-scale investment in multi-concept FEC development. EVO's model combines bowling, movies, gaming, and dining under one roof, making this a notable expansion for one of the sector's fastest-growing operators. FEC operators should take note of EVO's aggressive real estate strategy and mixed-use entertainment positioning.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Atlanta Closing, ArieForce One Roller Coaster Future Uncertain
articles.summary: Fun Spot America's Atlanta location is closing, leaving the fate of its highly anticipated ArieForce One RMC coaster in question. The closure highlights the operational and financial challenges facing independent FEC and amusement park operators in competitive markets. This is a cautionary case study for operators weighing capital-intensive attraction investments.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Ufenau Backs Eco Kart to Build Indoor Go-Kart Entertainment Platform
articles.summary: European private equity firm Ufenau Capital Partners is backing Eco Kart to develop a multi-site indoor go-kart entertainment platform, signaling growing investor appetite for scalable FEC concepts. The deal underscores how electric go-kart venues are attracting institutional capital as a roll-up opportunity. FEC investors and operators should watch this as a model for platform-building in the kart racing segment.

articles.title: AAMA FEC of the Year Award Applications Closing Soon
articles.summary: The American Amusement Machine Association is reminding FEC operators that applications for its prestigious FEC of the Year award are closing imminently. Winning or being nominated for this award carries significant industry recognition and marketing value. Operators who haven't yet applied should act quickly.

articles.title: Market Report: Key Segments Shaping the Family and Indoor Entertainment Center Industry
articles.summary: A new market analysis breaks down the major segments driving growth in the family and indoor entertainment center sector, offering data useful for strategic planning. The report covers trends across attractions, food and beverage, technology, and demographics. FEC operators and investors will find this useful for benchmarking and identifying expansion opportunities.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market: Emerging Trends and Leading Players
articles.summary: A new industry report examines the structure and emerging trends within the location-based entertainment market, spotlighting key operators and technology providers shaping the sector. The analysis covers VR, immersive attractions, and multi-concept venues as growth drivers. FEC operators and suppliers will find the competitive intelligence valuable for positioning.

articles.title: India's Indoor Entertainment Sector Gains Momentum as Business Opportunity
articles.summary: India's indoor entertainment and FEC sector is experiencing accelerating growth, driven by rising middle-class spending and urbanization. The piece highlights expanding opportunities for international operators and suppliers looking to enter or scale in the Indian market. This is relevant context for FEC brands and equipment suppliers evaluating international growth strategies.

articles.title: Intercard Cashless System Deployed at 23rd Speedpark Location in France
articles.summary: Intercard has continued its expansion with France's Speedpark chain, installing its cashless card and game management system at the operator's 23rd location. The deal reinforces Intercard's dominance in the European FEC technology market and highlights the trend toward multi-site cashless payment deployments. FEC operators evaluating cashless systems should note this as a strong reference case.

articles.title: VISIT Unifies Nordic Hospitality Tech Portfolio Under Single Platform for Attractions Operators
articles.summary: VISIT, formerly the Visit Group, has rebranded and consolidated its suite of digital tools into a unified platform covering sales, operations, and the visitor journey for attractions and hospitality clients. The company serves over 10,000 clients across hotels, experiences, and destinations. FEC operators seeking an integrated tech stack for ticketing, operations, and guest experience should watch this platform closely.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's family entertainment center is investing in a multi-level go-kart attraction, a format that has proven highly effective at driving repeat visits and differentiation in competitive markets. Multi-level kart tracks require significant capital but deliver strong guest engagement and social media visibility. This is a useful benchmarking example for FEC operators considering kart upgrades.

articles.title: Former Retail Store Converts to Family Action Park in Johnson County, Kansas
articles.summary: A former craft store in Johnson County is being transformed into a family action park, reflecting the ongoing trend of retail-to-entertainment conversions as vacant big-box spaces attract FEC concepts. The project illustrates how operators are capitalizing on affordable large-format retail real estate to build out multi-attraction venues. This adaptive reuse trend is worth tracking for operators and real estate teams scouting new locations.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley & Eats Opens Delaware's Largest Indoor Entertainment Destination in Newark
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley & Eats has opened what is being billed as Delaware's largest indoor entertainment venue in Newark, combining bowling, dining, and other attractions. The opening is another example of the full-service eatertainment model gaining traction in secondary markets. FEC operators can use this as a case study in combining food, beverage, and attractions for broader demographic appeal.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Billed as Unlike Any Other Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park has opened in Atlanta, positioning itself as a unique offering in the competitive metro market. The venue's marketing emphasizes differentiation, a critical strategy as indoor entertainment options multiply in major cities. FEC operators in urban markets can draw lessons from this venue's positioning and attraction mix.

articles.title: Big Air Trampoline Park Prepares for Opening Weekend in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air is opening a new trampoline park location in Glendale, Arizona, adding to the brand's growing national footprint. The opening reflects continued demand for active entertainment venues in the Southwest market. Trampoline park operators and suppliers should monitor Big Air's expansion trajectory.

articles.title: The Rush Funplex Expanding to Mesa, Arizona
articles.summary: The Rush Funplex is bringing its multi-attraction indoor entertainment concept to Mesa, Arizona, continuing the brand's regional expansion. The move reflects strong consumer demand for family entertainment in the Phoenix metro area. FEC operators eyeing the Southwest should note the competitive landscape filling in rapidly.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Opens Glow-in-the-Dark Venue in Denton, Texas
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf has opened a new glow-in-the-dark mini golf location in Denton, Texas, expanding its franchise footprint in the Dallas-Fort Worth market. The brand's themed, blacklight format continues to resonate with families and groups seeking novelty entertainment. Mini golf operators and prospective franchisees can look to Monster Mini Golf's growth as a franchise model reference.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. Plans New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. is bringing a new indoor entertainment concept to Keller, Texas, targeting the growing suburban Fort Worth market. Details suggest a social entertainment format combining activities with food and beverage. The Dallas-Fort Worth corridor continues to be a hotbed for new FEC development, making this a market to watch.

articles.title: X Golf Alliance Opens Golf Simulator Venue with Food and Drinks in North Fort Worth
articles.summary: X Golf Alliance has opened a golf simulator venue featuring multiple bays, a full food menu, and a bar in north Fort Worth, adding to the rapidly growing simulator golf category. The format appeals to a wide demographic and benefits from relatively low space requirements compared to full FEC buildouts. Operators and investors tracking the golf entertainment segment will want to follow X Golf's expansion.

articles.title: Hasbro Expands into Experiential Entertainment with The Play District Project
articles.summary: Hasbro is entering the experiential entertainment space with The Play District, a new concept bringing its iconic toy and game IP to physical venue experiences. The move by a major toy brand into location-based entertainment signals how IP holders are seeking new revenue streams through FEC-style activations. FEC operators should watch how established consumer brands compete and potentially partner in the experiential space.

articles.title: Columbus' Last Magic Mountain Entertainment Center Closing Its Doors
articles.summary: The final Magic Mountain family entertainment center location in Columbus is permanently closing, ending a long-running regional chain's presence in the market. The closure reflects the challenges facing older, single-concept FEC facilities competing against newer, multi-attraction venues. This is a reminder for operators to continuously invest in refreshing their attraction mix and guest experience.

articles.title: Belt Entertainment & Volleys FEC Announces Permanent Closure
articles.summary: Belt Entertainment & Volleys, a family entertainment center combining bowling and other activities, has announced it is closing its doors permanently. The closure adds to a pattern of FEC consolidation and attrition as the market becomes increasingly competitive. Operators should review this as a case study in the risks facing mid-size venues without clear differentiation.

articles.title: London-Based Interactive Sports Entertainment Venue Opens in Paris
articles.summary: A London-originated indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food has opened its first Paris location. The venue represents the European expansion of socially-oriented active entertainment concepts that blend sports simulation with food and beverage. FEC operators tracking the social sports entertainment trend should monitor this format's international traction.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Venue in Greater Noida, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has launched a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, India, tapping into the country's growing appetite for organized indoor family entertainment. The opening reflects the broader momentum of India's FEC market as mall-based entertainment expands. Suppliers and operators with international ambitions should note India's accelerating development pace.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Research: Parents in 29 Countries Limit Birthday Party Guest Lists
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has released a 29-country consumer research study on birthday party planning behavior, finding that parents commonly cap guest lists due to cost and logistical concerns. The data has direct implications for FEC birthday party package pricing, group size structuring, and marketing messaging. Operators running birthday party programs should review these insights to better align offerings with parent expectations.

articles.title: Laser Tag and Combat Ops FEC Hosts Fundraiser for Local Veterans
articles.summary: A Combat Ops laser tag entertainment center hosted a fundraiser benefiting local veterans, demonstrating how FEC venues can build community goodwill and local press coverage through cause-related programming. Community partnership events are an effective low-cost marketing strategy for driving awareness among new customer segments. FEC operators should consider similar initiatives to strengthen local ties.

articles.title: Arizona Ranks Its Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has ranked the top 10 indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, offering a useful snapshot of which FEC concepts and venues are resonating with consumers in a major Sun Belt market. The list highlights the diversity of formats now competing for indoor entertainment spend, from escape rooms to trampoline parks and golf simulators. FEC operators can use rankings like this to benchmark their visibility and reputation in regional markets.

articles.title: Mall of America Adds Immersive Bubble Attraction
articles.summary: Mall of America has added a new immersive bubble-themed attraction to its entertainment lineup, reflecting the continued appetite for novel, Instagrammable experiences within retail and entertainment destinations. The addition illustrates how major entertainment anchors are layering immersive concepts to drive foot traffic and repeat visits. FEC operators inside or adjacent to retail centers can look to this as a model for pop-up or permanent experience additions.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens in Frisco, Texas — A New Regional FEC-Style Model
articles.summary: Universal Destinations & Experiences has officially opened Universal Kids Resort in Frisco, Texas, its first regional theme park specifically designed for children ages 3 to 8. The park represents a significant new competitive format targeting the family entertainment market in suburban locations, blurring the line between theme park and FEC. Regional FEC operators in similar suburban markets should assess how a major brand entering the kids entertainment space may affect guest traffic and expectations.

articles.title: Evel Knievel Interactive Museum Opens in Downtown Las Vegas
articles.summary: The Evel Knievel Experience, a new interactive museum and attraction, has opened in Las Vegas' Arts District, offering an immersive look at the legendary daredevil's life and legacy. The venue is an example of IP-driven experiential entertainment designed to appeal to both nostalgia-seeking adults and younger guests. FEC operators and attraction designers can draw inspiration from how entertainment-focused museums are integrating interactive and themed elements.
